title: Sahel Insurgency Update — Apr 06, 2026
summary: JNIM detonated two IEDs on a joint FAMa/Africa Corps patrol between Kugo and Tonka in Timbuktu Region on April 6, killing at least one soldier. On April 1, Tuareg separatist group FLA used FPV kamikaze drones to destroy a Russian Africa Corps camp near Tessalit/Amachach in the Kidal region, while a separate report documented civilian abuse allegations linked to Africa Corps operations across Mali. FAMa's Operation Dugukoloko in the Kidal theater concluded April 3, with an April 2 airstrike reportedly killing dozens of militants southeast of Kidal. In Niger, Boko Haram kidnapped seven Chadian nationals (killing one) near the Niger-Chad border, while IS-Sahel attacked the Niger-Benin oil pipeline infrastructure. Three new map-lines document the FLA drone attack vector, FAMa airstrike corridor, and JNIM IED patrol ambush route.

title: Sahel Insurgency Tracker Update — Sat Apr 4, 2026
summary: Islamic State Sahel Province (IS-Sahel) attacked and sabotaged a section of the Niger-Benin international oil pipeline infrastructure in western Niger. Boko Haram Demands 500M CFA per Hostage for Kidnapped Chadian Nationals.

title: Sahel Insurgency Tracker Update — Fri Apr 3, 2026
summary: Boko Haram fighters abducted seven Chadian nationals in Niger's border region, killing one of the hostages.

title: Sahel Insurgency Update — Apr 02, 2026
summary: Human Rights Watch released a major report documenting 1,837 verified civilian killings in Burkina Faso between January 2023 and August 2025 — over 1,200 attributed to government forces and VDP militias, the remainder to JNIM and IS-Sahel — with the Traoré junta rejecting the findings. In Mali, FAMa's information directorate (DIRPA) publicly denied on March 31 that any prisoner release was made in exchange for JNIM opening a fuel corridor, even as diesel prices remain 29.7% higher than pre-blockade levels. The Front de Libération de l'Azawad (FLA) conducted its seventh claimed drone and missile strike in March on FAMa and Africa Corps positions at Anéfis, Kidal region on March 30, continuing Tuareg asymmetric resistance following the November 2023 fall of Kidal. JNIM also assassinated a youth association leader in Tonka, Timbuktu Region on March 27 — consistent with the group's pattern of targeting civil society figures perceived as collaborating with security forces.

title: Sahel Insurgency Tracker Update — Wed Apr 1, 2026
summary: The Front de Libération de l'Azawad (FLA) conducted an FPV kamikaze drone strike that destroyed a Russian Africa Corps camp in the Kidal region of northern Mali. A report published April 1, 2026 documented widespread fear and insecurity in Malian communities where Russian Africa Corps personnel operate alongside Malian Armed Forces (FAMa).

title: Sahel Insurgency Tracker Update — Tue Mar 31, 2026
summary: FAMa Officially Denies Releasing JNIM Detainees in Exchange for Fuel Corridor.

title: Sahel Insurgency Tracker Update — Mon Mar 30, 2026
summary: Tuareg FLA Launches Drone and Missile Strike on FAMa-Africa Corps Base at Anéfis. Russia Transfers Military Equipment to FAMa at Bamako Senou Airbase.

title: Sahel Insurgency Update — Mar 29, 2026
summary: IS-Sahel killed 6 Niger Army soldiers in an attack on positions at Sanama, Tillabéri Region on March 25, reinforcing the group's sustained offensive pressure in western Niger's Liptako-Gourma zone. A new map-line documents the Sanama attack vector. A Malian Armed Forces patrol briefly crossed into Mauritanian territory occupying six villages before withdrawing after diplomatic negotiations — an incident attributed to GPS/border demarcation confusion with no casualties. The JNIM fuel blockade resolution (March 23) was confirmed with diesel prices reaching 940 CFA/liter (+29.7%), updating the contested blockade claim's resolution status. The conflict has now entered its 15th year since the initial MNLA offensive in January 2012.

title: Sahel Insurgency Update — Mar 29, 2026
summary: FAMa conducted a major airstrike on an IS-Sahel base northwest of Ménaka on March 26, claiming 50+ militants neutralized and an entire logistics camp destroyed — the third Malian engagement in the Ménaka theater in one week. IS-Sahel responded on March 27 with a motorcycle ambush on a FAMa patrol at Inkadevan north of Ménaka, with FAMa helicopter gunships striking retreating fighters. On March 22, JNIM killed at least 14 soldiers in an assault on a military post in Bagade, northern Burkina Faso — part of an escalating JNIM offensive that has killed 120+ security forces in Burkina since February. The economic impact of the Mali–JNIM fuel deal became clear on March 28 when Bamako announced diesel prices had surged 29.7% to 940 CFA francs/liter following the end of the blockade. ECOWAS on March 26 appointed former Guinean Prime Minister Lansana Kouyaté as chief mediator to resume dialogue with the AES states, the third such appointment after predecessors failed to achieve a breakthrough.

title: Sahel Insurgency Tracker Update — Sat Mar 28, 2026
summary: Mali's government announced significant fuel price increases on March 28, as supply chains resumed following the March 23 prisoner-for-fuel-corridor deal with JNIM.

title: Sahel Insurgency Tracker Update — Fri Mar 27, 2026
summary: Islamic State – Wilayat Sahel (IS-Sahel) fighters mounted on motorcycles attacked a Malian Armed Forces (FAMa) patrol in the Inkadevan area, north of Ménaka in eastern Mali. JNIM Assassinates Youth Organization Leader in Tonka, Timbuktu Region.

title: Sahel Insurgency Update — Mar 26, 2026
summary: Mali's military junta struck a highly controversial deal around March 23, releasing over 100 jihadist detainees to end JNIM's seven-month fuel blockade of Bamako — a truce that analysts warn sets a dangerous precedent for economic warfare as a jihadist negotiating tool. Malian Armed Forces (FAMa), supported by Africa Corps air assets, conducted ground and air operations in the Sikasso, Ségou, and Mopti regions on March 22–24, neutralizing multiple militants in coordinated strikes. Guinea ramped up counter-terrorism activity by dismantling a GSIM/JNIM-linked network on March 23–24, arresting at least 11 suspects in eastern border operations and charging individuals tied to online radicalization networks. Analysts issued fresh warnings on March 25 that JNIM's insurgency is spreading southward into coastal West Africa, with active recruitment underway in Benin, Togo, Côte d'Ivoire, and Ghana via Fulani ethnic networks. The JNIM–ISSP inter-jihadist rivalry continued with reported clashes in Burkina Faso as both groups competed for territorial dominance following February's major coordinated offensives.

title: Sahel Insurgency Tracker Update — Wed Mar 25, 2026
summary: Islamic State – Wilayat Sahel (IS-Sahel) claimed responsibility for an attack on Niger Army positions in Sanama, Tillabéri Region, killing 6 soldiers. Security analysts and regional media issued warnings that JNIM's jihadist insurgency is migrating southward from the Sahel core toward coastal West Africa, placing the entire Gulf of Guinea at risk.

title: Sahel Insurgency Tracker Update — Tue Mar 24, 2026
summary: Guinean armed forces arrested 11 individuals suspected of links to JNIM cells during a counter-terrorism operation in the east of the country, in border areas adjacent to Mali. The Malian Air Force conducted airstrikes against militant positions in central Mali, targeting armed groups in the Mopti region.

title: Sahel Insurgency Tracker Update — Mon Mar 23, 2026
summary: Mali Releases 100+ Jihadist Detainees to End JNIM Fuel Blockade. Guinea Dismantles GSIM/JNIM Terrorist Network, Arrests Multiple Suspects.

title: Sahel Insurgency Tracker Update — Sun Mar 22, 2026
summary: JNIM Kills 14+ Soldiers in Attack on Bagade Military Post, Burkina Faso. The Malian Armed Forces (FAMa) conducted two large-scale coordinated counter-terrorism operations on March 22.
